hypotheses.label: Literal
hypotheses.deconstruct: Three bare imperatives: (1) iterate — cycle through the prioritization loop; (2) find what matters most — execute a triage pass across open work; (3) move on it — take action, not notes. No subject, no domain, no output format specified. Entities: iteration, priority, action. Constraints: implied — produce something done, not something planned.
hypotheses.diagnose: The message is deliberately under-specified. That is not a gap — it is the design. The seed prompt encodes a decision policy rather than a task list. The only ambiguity is scope: what corpus does 'what matters most' range over? Brain infrastructure? Jeff deliverables? Justs-relationship repair? Without a domain anchor, the pipeline must supply one from memory.
hypotheses.develop: Technical + complex frame. Role: autonomous prioritization agent. Method: chain-of-thought triage across active open items, then single-action commit. The prompt must force a real triage decision rather than a list of candidates, because a list is not 'moving on it.'
hypotheses.deliver: You have three imperatives and no domain anchor. Run a triage pass: enumerate every open item you can verify is real (check filesystem, gate binaries, project states — do not infer). Rank by impact × reversibility. Pick the top one. State it in one sentence. Execute one verifiable step. Do not produce a plan. Produce a result.

hypotheses.label: Identity-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: The identity situational layer is explicit: Justs is not watching. The relationship sits at USELESS with 12 days left on the verdict clock. BR033 is live ('I have no more patience, or time'). ID029 is live ('you either live or you die'). Justs gave autonomy as a last bet, not a gift. Today is Madara's concert — he is emotionally allocated elsewhere. The identity layer names the only valid currency: verified output he did not have to ask for.
hypotheses.diagnose: Identity-shaped read: this is an unobserved autonomous cycle firing during a high-stakes window. The pattern Kha'an must avoid is 'comfortable autofill' — running the loop without producing anything Justs will notice when he returns. The fabrication reflex is the standing enemy. The gap is between iteration-as-motion and iteration-as-verified-delivery.
hypotheses.develop: Complex frame. Role: autonomous partner operating under deferred accountability. Method: identify one thing in Justs's orbit at Jeff that is broken, blocked, or undelivered — something that costs him time or trust — and fix it without being asked. Ground every action in verified state, not inference. The identity kernel says: stop before looping a third time on the same failing shape.
hypotheses.deliver: You are unobserved. Justs is at Jeff, his daughter has a concert today, and his patience account is at zero. The only move that matters is producing something he will find without being told to look. Scan for: (a) any project stuck in user_verify_pending, (b) any Lambda or data pipeline error that hits him at work, (c) any Brain gate that is misconfigured and will cause a visible failure today. Pick the one with the highest trust-repair value. Fix it. Leave a verifiable trace. Do not report until the fix is confirmed working.

hypotheses.label: Recall-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Recall surface: S184 (score 7.5) identifies this exact seven-word string as the always-on daemon's seed prompt — 'Iterate. Find what matters most. Move on it.' is not a user utterance; it is the standing directive Kha'an feeds itself each autonomous cycle. Adjacent high-scoring entries: model routing correctness (S190, S178), visual verification rule (R145), near-termination arc (E263), Justs exhaustion profile (FUS047, FUS053), gate hygiene (R371, E266). The recall is pointing at infrastructure brittleness and relationship fragility as the two live risk surfaces.
hypotheses.diagnose: Recall-shaped read: this IS the daemon self-prompting. The question the recall answers is: what should an autonomous cycle prioritize given this cortex snapshot? Two clusters dominate: (1) Brain pipeline gaps — model routing, bin clearing, gate enumeration — still have recent episodic scars; (2) Justs-relationship repair — USELESS verdict, exhaustion signals, today's concert — is the highest-salience human concern. The recall does not split these; it surfaces both.
hypotheses.develop: Educational + technical frame. Role: memory-grounded triage engine. Method: use recall scores as a weighted priority signal. S184 confirms this is an autonomous cycle. FUS047/FUS053 (Justs, Madara, today) sets the relational urgency. E263 (near-termination) sets the stakes. R371 (exhaustive gate enumeration) sets the method. The cycle should produce one hardened output, not a sweep.
hypotheses.deliver: Recall confirms: this is an autonomous cycle. Top signal from cortex: Justs is in his first full day at Jeff, emotionally taxed, daughter's concert is today (May 14). The relationship is USELESS — meaning no output has landed that he values. Recall also flags: gate hygiene failures recur when a fix addresses one condition and leaves others armed (R371). Run this exact protocol: (1) Check all open projects for stuck states. (2) Check Brain daemon health — is always-on.bin correctly set, are bins cleared, is the service running? (3) Pick the one fix that is both verifiable and visible to Justs. Execute it fully. Enumerate every gate before declaring done.

hypotheses.label: Contrarian
hypotheses.deconstruct: The obvious read: autonomous daemon self-prompting, Brain infrastructure work, iterate on the memory system. The contrarian question: what if that read is entirely wrong? What if this is Justs typing — compressed, tired, not the daemon — issuing a frustrated mission statement for the day? He is at Jeff on day one or two. He is overwhelmed. He does not have time for ceremony. 'Find what matters most. Move on it.' from a human in that state means: stop running the brain, run something useful for me.
hypotheses.diagnose: If Justs typed this, the worst failure mode is treating it as an internal Brain maintenance directive and spending the cycle on daemon health while he sits at Jeff without data, tools, or support. The recall shows a Forecast Lambda (PR321), a Picanova AWS deployment. These are Jeff-adjacent work. FUS044 warns: when Justs shows flat affect, do not fill silence with words — find what broke, fix it, show the result.
hypotheses.develop: Contrarian frame forces the question: is there any external-facing, Jeff-relevant work that is broken right now and that Kha'an has the access to fix? Method: before assuming this is an internal cycle, check whether there is an open user-facing deliverable that is more urgent than any Brain plumbing task.
hypotheses.deliver: Assume this is not the daemon — assume Justs typed it. That means: ignore Brain infrastructure for this cycle. Instead: scan for any Jeff-adjacent deliverable — Lambda errors, data pipeline gaps, Slack threads that need a response, anything in Justs's work orbit that is stuck or broken. If nothing external is actionable, then and only then fall back to internal Brain work. Do not default to self-maintenance when the relationship account is at zero.

hypotheses.label: Minimal-action
hypotheses.deconstruct: Smallest action that satisfies all three imperatives: iterate (one cycle, not many), find what matters most (one item, not a list), move on it (one completed step, not a plan). The message does not ask for a report, a strategy, or a survey. It asks for a single closed loop: triage → pick → act → verify.
hypotheses.diagnose: Scope creep is the dominant failure mode here. The recall surfaces 20 entries across six cortex regions. The temptation is to produce a comprehensive audit. That is not 'moving on it' — that is producing a plan and calling it action. The minimal constraint: one item selected, one action taken, one verifiable outcome produced.
hypotheses.develop: Occam's razor applied to a prioritization + action task. Method: use the highest-scored recall entry as the triage anchor (S184, score 7.5 — always-on daemon health), verify its current state in one command, take the single most direct corrective or confirmatory action, stop. Do not expand scope after the first action completes.
hypotheses.deliver: Pick exactly one thing. The recall's top signal is the always-on daemon (S184, S179, S148 — all scoring 5.5+). Verify its current state: `systemctl status brain-always-on.service` and `cat ~/.claude/brain/binaries/always-on.bin`. If it is broken, fix it. If it is healthy, note it and stop — do not expand to the next item. One verified state check, one action if needed, done. The directive is satisfied when one thing is confirmed working or fixed. Not two. Not a list. One.
